column3: High-level grouping associating multiple sampling events within the same research, conservation, catalogue, exhibition, or documentation context.

An individual act of removing material from a Heritage Object at a specific Sample Site. Serves as the authoritative mediator between object, site, and sample; any direct object–sample links are secondary and non-authoritative.

A digital image used for documentation and annotation of the Heritage Object. Acts as the primary coordinate reference frame for image-space Sampling Points (E94), including future IIIF selector alignment.
A curated physical artefact that contains/carries the sampled matter (S13). P46 is used here as a pragmatic 'contains/carries' proxy for collection management purposes.
The removed matter, treated as an amount of matter (CRMsci S13) whose identity is grounded in material continuity and representativity.

A conceptual sampling location on the Heritage Object. A single Sample Site may be defined multiple times via P168 by multiple Sampling Points (E94) taken from different images/regions and/or later re-annotations; these are alternative representation-scoped anchors for the same site, not distinct physical sites.

column1: // Replace with links to physical sample E22 - see below

column3: A new curated Physical Sample identity created as the result of a Sample Splitting event. It represents a distinct registered sample entity derived from the source sample’s material (S13), with its own persistent identity, storage, status, and lifecycle history, while remaining materially traceable to the original sample via the splitting event.

A new Sample created through a Sample Splitting event. Although shown here only in relation to the splitting activity, this sub-sample is a full S13 Sample and may independently be observed, measured, modified, represented by images, and further split. These standard Sample relationships are omitted from the diagram for clarity.

A derived digital region (crop/ROI) created for documentation or annotation purposes. Represents a Sample Site in a specific image but is not the site itself. Used as a contextual or classificatory aid for locating sampling points, particularly in annotation-driven workflows (e.g. ResearchSpace, IIIF viewers).

column1: //To actually define a sample point relative to a region we would need something like

column3: A representation-scoped spatial primitive expressed in the coordinate system of the referenced digital image/region (e.g., pixel coordinates). When linked via P168 it defines the conceptual locus of the Sample Site only in that representational frame (not object-space, not metrological). Also functions as a placeholder for future IIIF selector/annotation alignment. Coordinates are interpreted in the coordinate system of the referenced full image. A link to an image region may also be recorded when the point was created via a derived crop/region (e.g., ResearchSpace annotation workflow).

Pixel or image-space coordinate value automatically generated from the Sampling Point (E94) relative to the referenced digital image. These coordinates describe the location in the image’s coordinate system, not the physical position on the original Heritage Object. Physical/object-space coordinates, when recorded, are captured separately as manual metadata.

column1: //Optional direct connections between Objects and Samples

column3: An event in which one or more quantitative properties of the sample were measured.
An event in which qualitative or visual properties of the sample were examined
An event that altered the sample after its initial removal, such as preparation, embedding, or coating.

column1: //Dual-layer: Curated Sample Item (object) that carries/contains the S13 material sample
//Sample Site is the former location of the curated sample item (optional but often useful)
//Optional: current storage location of the curated sample item

column3: A current storage place/location for the curated sample item (e.g. store room, cabinet, drawer, institution).

column1: //Dual-layer for sub-sample: new curated item for the new S13
S13: Material Sample#-1 P46i_forms_part_of (1 to 1) E22: Physical Sample#-1
